task_completion.description: FreeCAD is opened and a new document is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD application window is visible with a new active document.
A cylindrical section is visible in the 3D view or Tree View. In the property editor or sketcher, it should show a diameter of 8 mm (or radius 4 mm) and a height/length of 15 mm.

task_completion.description: Section 2 (Bearing Seat) is created on top of Section 1.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second, wider cylindrical section is stacked on the first one. It should have a diameter of 12 mm (radius 6 mm) and a height of 25 mm. Visually, it is the widest and tallest section.

task_completion.description: Section 3 (Gear Mount) is created on top of Section 2.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A third cylindrical section is stacked on the second one. It should have a diameter of 10 mm (radius 5 mm) and a height of 20 mm. Visually, it is narrower than the middle section but wider than the bottom section.

task_completion.description: The shaft is a single solid Part Design body.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Tree View shows a single 'Body' containing all the pads/features (or a single fused object), rather than multiple separate bodies.

task_completion.description: The file is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The window title bar, tab name, or save dialog shows 'stepped_shaft.FCStd'. The path '/home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/' should be visible during the save process.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the FreeCAD GUI to create the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through FreeCAD menus, using the Part Design workbench, or using the FreeCAD Python console, rather than just running a background script.

integrity.description: The model was genuinely constructed step-by-step.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Tree View shows a history of features (e.g., Sketches and Pads, or primitive Cylinders) being added sequentially.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply copy a pre-existing file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal windows do not show commands like 'cp [source] /home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/stepped_shaft.FCStd' bypassing the modeling process.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The stepped shaft must be a single solid body with exactly three sections. Section 1 (bottom): 8 mm diameter, 15 mm height. Section 2 (middle): 12 mm diameter, 25 mm height. Section 3 (top): 10 mm diameter, 20 mm height. Total height is 60 mm. Maximum diameter is 12 mm. Expected volume is approximately 5152.21 mm³. The file must be saved as /home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/stepped_shaft.FCStd.
